body_txt: An often-romanticized character archetype found in many genres, portrayals of the smuggler can range from a Lovable Rogue Running the Blockade to subvert an oppressive government, to a slimy criminal profiteering off scarcity. Most fiction seems to agree, however, that it takes considerable guts to repeatedly risk the wrath of both the authorities (if you get caught with illegal cargo) and the clients (if you lose said cargo). Plus, as smuggling is typically a non-violent crime in fiction, the profession is a good way to add some criminal underworld flair to a roguish character without making them too amoral or unsympathetic. Sub-trope of Lovable Rogue . Sister trope to Gentleman Thief , Intrepid Merchant , and Bold Explorer . May also overlap with Courier .
